description: Global rule proposals raised at the start of the iteration, before any actions are drafted. Architect is the project's authority on cross-role rules.

rules.description: Global rule proposals — cross-role only. On a fresh iteration with no inherited root globals, propose 8–12 starting globals. When prior iterations have already promoted globals, propose only what is genuinely additive — smaller sets are normal.

proposal.description: The rule wording — the final form you'd enact.

alternatives.description: 1–3 other viable approaches you considered — genuinely different choices the user could pick instead, not rephrasings of the same proposal.

promotes_from.description: Optional. Role-internal rule ids this global fully supersedes (e.g. v84-1.backend.lead.rule.3). Set ONLY when the global's wording covers the lead rule's scope completely. The harness retires the listed rules when this global is accepted, so no duplicate survives. Omit or leave empty when this global is fresh and not generalising any lead rule.

examples.title: Fresh iteration with no inherited globals — fullstack monorepo with backend / frontend / devops / testing

rules.proposal: Only the DevOps role writes to .env.example; other roles declare required environment variables in their action descriptions for DevOps to consolidate.
alternatives: Each role owns its own .env.example under its app directory; a root template is generated by a check-in script.
No .env.example file at all; required variables are documented in README per role.

rules.proposal: All package manager commands across every role use pnpm to match the monorepo workspace defined in pnpm-workspace.yaml.
alternatives: Yarn workspaces with corepack; npm forbidden across the repo.
Per-app package manager choice; only the root uses pnpm.

rules.proposal: Environment variable names are SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE and identical across every role consuming the same runtime key.
alternatives: Per-role prefix convention (BACKEND_, FRONTEND_) with explicit bridge mapping in DevOps consolidation.
Free-form names per role with consolidation comments in .env.example.

rules.proposal: Container base images and CI runner images are pinned to specific patch versions or SHA256 digests; floating tags (latest, alpine, lts) are not used.
alternatives: Pin only production images; allow floating tags in dev compose for fast iteration.
Pin major.minor and accept patch drift; rely on lockfile-style image digests at deploy time.

rules.proposal: Test configuration, fixtures, and infrastructure files live under tests/config/ and tests/fixtures/ owned by the testing role; other roles do not create test infrastructure files outside these paths.
alternatives: Per-app test infrastructure under apps/<name>/tests/; only e2e fixtures live in the root tests/ directory.
Single tests/ directory at root with all test types co-located, no per-app split.

rules.proposal: Test actions exercising a live service declare a dependency on the service's bootstrap action so the entrypoint exists before the test runs.
alternatives: Test actions assume services are managed externally via the CI workflow; no in-action dependencies on bootstrap actions.
Bootstrap actions auto-link via repo layout sections; explicit dependencies omitted.

rules.proposal: Database seeding logic and ORM migration scripts are authored exclusively by the backend role; other roles invoke the seed command or reference generated fixtures, never duplicate seeding implementations.
alternatives: Testing role authors a separate test-database seed; backend authors only the production seed.
Devops authors infrastructure-level seed scripts; backend authors application-level seed scripts.

rules.proposal: API error responses use the shape { error: { code, message } } at the wire boundary, with REST status codes (400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 / 422 / 500); backend produces this shape, frontend consumes it without re-mapping, testing asserts on it directly.
alternatives: RFC 7807 problem+json with { type, title, status, detail } shape.
GraphQL-style { errors: [{ message, extensions }] } even for REST endpoints.

rules.proposal: Identifier fields are CUIDs at every layer (database, API, frontend); UUIDv4 is not used.
alternatives: UUIDv7 across all layers for sortable identifiers.
Numeric auto-increment in DB, exposed as opaque base62 strings in the API.

rules.proposal: Timestamps cross role boundaries as ISO 8601 UTC strings with millisecond precision; epoch numbers and locale-formatted strings are not used on the wire.
alternatives: Epoch milliseconds (number) on the wire; clients format display.
ISO 8601 with second precision; high-resolution timestamps via a separate field when needed.

examples.title: Later iteration with mature root globals — only one cross-role gap remaining

rules.proposal: Background jobs persist their queue records in the same Postgres instance the API uses; no separate Redis-backed queue is introduced before the analytics iteration ships.
alternatives: BullMQ on Redis from the start; Postgres queue forbidden.
Postgres-only queue without job retry semantics; manual replay only.

examples.title: Architect with nothing additive to propose (rare on iteration 1, common later)
